context: 1960 was a monumental year for Hindi cinema, with the release of K. Asif's epic Mughal-e-Azam, which placed two songs in the top 10. Mohammed Rafi dominated the charts with his extraordinary vocal range. The year reflected the golden age of playback singing, with composers like Naushad, Ravi, and Roshan creating elaborate orchestral arrangements that defined Bollywood's classical period.

tracks.title: Mujhko Is Raat Ki Tanhai Mein
Jab Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya
Chal Ri Sajani Ab Kya Soche
Tere Pyaar Ka Aasra Chahta Hu
Mohabbat Ki Jhuthi Kahani Pe Roye
Tu Hai Harjai To Apna Bhi Yehi Taur Sahi
